quotes.text: If you're not embarrassed by the first version of your product, you've launched too late.
quotes.author: Reid Hoffman

quotes.text: Stop overthinking. Start shipping. The market will tell you what to build next.
quotes.author: Pieter Levels

quotes.text: Build 12 startups in 12 months. Most will fail. The ones that don't will change your life.
quotes.author: Pieter Levels

quotes.text: Charge more.
quotes.author: Patrick McKenzie (patio11)

quotes.text: Don't call yourself a programmer. Call yourself something that sounds like you make or save the company money.
quotes.author: Patrick McKenzie (patio11)

quotes.text: Do things that don't scale.
quotes.author: Paul Graham

quotes.text: If you have ramen profitability, you're default alive. The most important thing in a startup is not to die.
quotes.author: Paul Graham

quotes.text: If you want to set off and go develop some grand new thing, you don't need millions of dollars of capitalization. You need enough pizza and Diet Coke to stick in your refrigerator.
quotes.author: John Carmack

quotes.text: You're not Google. Your 3-person startup doesn't need microservices. It needs to ship.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Client: 'Can you just add one small feature?' Narrator: It was not, in fact, a small feature.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Shipping beats perfection. Every time. No exceptions. Ship the ugly thing.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Your SaaS idea is probably a spreadsheet. Ship the spreadsheet first.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Build the audience before you build the product. Selling to strangers is hard mode.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Step 1: Get ramen profitable. Step 2: There is no step 2. Everything else is a luxury.
quotes.author: Unknown
quotes.source: Indie hacker culture (term from Paul Graham)

quotes.text: I don't compete on features. I compete on speed. Ship in days, not months.
quotes.author: Marc Lou

quotes.text: Revenue is vanity, profit is sanity, cash is reality.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The best marketing strategy for a developer is to actually ship something useful and let people find it.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The best funding round is revenue.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: At a startup, CTO stands for 'Currently The Only developer.'
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Indie hackers don't have exit strategies. They have 'make enough money to not get a real job' strategies.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best indie hacker metric is revenue per hour of work. Everything else is a distraction.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The beauty of being a solo founder is that you can ship in 20 minutes what takes a committee 3 months. The curse is that you have to do everything.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Ship fast, charge early, iterate often. This is the indie hacker's trifecta. Pick all three.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best thing about building for yourself: no stakeholder meetings. The worst: you are the stakeholder.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Building in public is just showing your work. But it also means everyone sees your mistakes, your pivots, and your 3 AM commits.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The indie hacker's greatest superpower isn't coding. It's the ability to make decisions without asking permission.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your first $1k MRR feels like conquering the world. Your first $10k MRR feels like a Tuesday. The journey between is where you learn.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best marketing for an indie project is being useful. Be useful in forums, be useful on Twitter, be useful everywhere.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Side projects are the best way to learn. Main projects are the best way to earn. You need both.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The indie hacker's nightmare isn't failure. It's spending 2 years building something that gets 3 users.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best indie businesses are ones you can run from a laptop, in a coffee shop, while ignoring everyone around you.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every indie hacker who says 'I'll never hire' eventually hires someone. The smart ones wait until they absolutely have to.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best tech stack for an indie project is the one you can ship with today. Not the one you'll learn eventually.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Indie hacking is just freelancing with equity. Both involve wearing 47 hats. The equity part is just hope in disguise.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to launch is when you have something that works. The worst time is when you think it's perfect.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your first product taught you everything. Your second product teaches you what actually matters.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Saying 'I'm building a SaaS' is cooler than saying 'I'm building a website.' But they're the same thing.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best indie hackers are the ones who build things they personally use. They're their own best customer.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: One-person businesses are the future. Not because they're trendy, but because leverage makes it possible.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The most underrated indie hacker skill is copywriting. If you can write a clear landing page, you can sell anything.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Indie hackers: 'I want to build something that makes $10k MRR.' Reality: you build something that makes $100 MRR and you're thrilled.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to add Stripe is before you launch. You'd be amazed how many people don't launch because they can't figure out payments.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Ship it, then fix it. Nobody sees your bugs until you've told them where to look.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The difference between a side project and a business is revenue. The difference between a business and a sustainable business is margin.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to validate a SaaS idea is to sell it before you build it. If nobody buys, save yourself 6 months.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Solo founders don't lack ideas. They lack the discipline to ship one idea instead of starting five.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The indie hacker's calendar: Monday — plan. Tuesday — code. Wednesday — code. Thursday — code. Friday — panic about marketing.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every indie hacker has 100 ideas. The successful ones execute one.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best SaaS feature request is the one you can implement in an afternoon and charge for monthly.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Side projects are the best career insurance. They prove you can build things, not just talk about them.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The indie hacker's journey: idea → build → launch → crickets → iterate → crickets → pivot → crickets → ship better → $100 MRR.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your first product won't be your best. Your second won't either. Your tenth might be the one.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best indie projects are the ones you'd use even if nobody else did.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Solo founders don't fail because they can't code. They fail because they can't market.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to validate an indie project is to pre-sell it. If nobody pays, save yourself the build.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Indie hackers who ship weekly grow faster than indie hackers who ship quarterly. Ship more, learn faster.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to launch on Product Hunt is when you have a product people want to tell their friends about.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your first 100 users should be people you know personally. If you can't convince them, you can't convince strangers.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The indie hacker's biggest enemy isn't competition. It's shiny object syndrome. Pick one thing and ship it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Building a tool you'd pay for is easy. Getting others to pay for it is hard. The gap between these is where indie hackers live.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best indie businesses are built on boring niches. Boring niches have paying customers. Exciting niches have Twitter followers.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your stack doesn't matter. Your ship rate does. Ship weekly or don't ship at all.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to start monetizing is the day you launch. The worst is 'someday.'
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Indie hackers who talk to users grow faster than indie hackers who code in isolation.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best indie hack is the one you can maintain alone for 3 years. If it needs a team, it's not an indie hack.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to quit your job is when you have 6 months of runway. Not when you're fed up. Not when you're rich.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your first product failure isn't a failure. It's tuition. You just paid for a masterclass.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best indie businesses have one thing in common: the founder genuinely cares about the problem.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your side project is a sandbox. Play in it. Break things. Try weird ideas. That's the point.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to raise prices is when your customers say 'that's it?' when you tell them the price.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Building a product is easy. Finding customers is hard. The best founders do both.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to build in public is always. Transparency compounds like interest.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your first paying customer is worth more than your next 100 signups. Treat them like royalty.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The indie hacker's motto: ship fast, charge more, sleep well.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every indie hacker should learn one marketing skill: writing. Everything else is a bonus.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to grow an indie business is to make your existing customers happier. New customers come from happy ones.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your product is your resume. Build something people want to talk about.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to start an indie business is before you need the money. Build while you have a safety net.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Indie hackers who ship weekly have 10x the chances of finding product-market fit than those who ship quarterly.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to validate an idea is to charge for it before it exists. If nobody pays, you've saved months.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your landing page is your best salesperson. Make it work while you sleep.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The hardest part of being a solo founder isn't the technical work. It's the isolation.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to compete with big companies is to be faster, more personal, and more focused. You can't outspend them.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your first year in business is survival. Your second year is learning. Your third year is growth.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best indie businesses are built on one simple insight. Find yours and execute relentlessly.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The indie hacker's office is wherever their laptop is. The best office is a café with good WiFi.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your side project is your playground. Play. Experiment. Break things. That's how you learn.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best indie hackers are the ones who can switch between CEO and developer in 5 minutes.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your first $1k in revenue is the hardest. The next $9k is easier. The next $90k is harder. The next $900k is the hardest.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The indie hacker's biggest asset isn't their code. It's their taste. Build things with taste.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your side project is a marathon, not a sprint. Pace yourself.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to validate your indie project is to pre-sell it. If nobody pays, save yourself the build.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie business should be something you'd use even if nobody else did. Build for yourself first.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Ship weekly or don't ship at all. The gap between weekly and quarterly is the gap between learning and guessing.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie business is only as good as its worst user experience. Fix the leaks.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every failed product teaches you something that no successful product could.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best indie hacker tool is the one that saves you 10 hours a week. If it saves you 10 minutes, it's not worth building.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie business should be something you can explain to your parents. If you can't, simplify.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to validate an indie idea is to build a landing page, collect emails, and see who signs up.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your first indie product should solve YOUR problem. Not what you think others' problems are.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to launch on Indie Hackers is when you have a working product and a story to tell.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie project's first 100 users are the hardest. The next 900 are easier. Focus on the first 100.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best indie businesses are built on personal pain. Build what you wish existed.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your side project should be fun. If it's not fun, it's just another job.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best indie hackers ship fast and learn faster. Speed is their superpower.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie project doesn't need a perfect logo. It needs paying customers. Ship.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to grow an indie project is to make your users successful. Their success is your success.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie business should be built on boring, repetitive problems. Boring problems pay monthly.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to build an audience is to be consistently helpful. Not consistently promotional.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie project is a marathon. Pace yourself. Celebrate small wins.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to add monetization is when you have users who'd miss your product if it disappeared.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your most important metric as an indie hacker is revenue per hour worked. Optimize for that.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best indie hackers don't try to build the next unicorn. They build profitable lifestyle businesses.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie project should be good enough that users would be sad if it disappeared.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to start is to start small. One feature. One customer. One success.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie business should run without you eventually. Build systems, not tasks.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie project's biggest advantage is that you care more than anyone else.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to build an indie business is before you need the money. Build while you have a safety net.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie project should be something you're proud to show people. If you're not, iterate.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie business should be built on something you'd do even if nobody paid you. That's the foundation.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best time to start monetizing is on day one. Not 'someday.' Not 'after we have 10k users.' Day one.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie project's first $100 in revenue is worth more than your first 10,000 free users.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to learn business is to run one. No MBA needed.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie business should be simple enough to explain in one sentence. If it takes a paragraph, simplify.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie project should be something you'd use even if nobody else did.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie project is your baby. Take care of it. Feed it. Grow it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your first year is survival. Your second year is learning. Your third year is growth.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your indie project is a playground. Play. Experiment. Break things. That's how you learn.
quotes.author: based
